name: Search commodities (enter commodity code)
caption: Description of the goods
heading: Commodity
secondaryActions: Commodity tree chapter buttons (07, 08, 09, 10, 11, 12, 13, 14, 15, 17, 18, 19, 20, 21, 23, 39) — each a submit button that drills into that chapter

structure.text: Enter commodity code (input, inputmode=numeric, width one-third)
Find the commodity in the commodity tree
Bespoke commodity tree: <ul class=commodity-tree> of chapter rows, each a code button + description button (submit buttons posting parent_NN)

govukComponents.usage: Section caption 'Description of the goods' above the H1
H3 'Find the commodity in the commodity tree'
Label 'Enter commodity code' for the commodity code input
'All commodities' breadcrumb root link (also carries custom class all-sections-link)

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Custom container styling the search box and the commodity-tree panel
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Bespoke IPAFFS layout wrapper; no GOV.UK equivalent class. Purely presentational.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: Standard govuk grid + spacing utilities; no dedicated component needed

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Bespoke commodity-tree browser: a breadcrumb ('All commodities' > chapter > ...) plus a list of chapter/heading nodes the user drills into by clicking. Each node is rendered inside <nav aria-label='Buttons section'>.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Entirely custom widget — the core alternative-to-search navigation for finding a commodity code by browsing the CN/commodity hierarchy. Heavy, non-standard, and posts back the whole form on every drill-down (server-rendered tree, no client component). This is the single biggest non-govuk pattern on the page.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: No direct GOV.UK component. Rebuild could use an accessible-autocomplete on the code/description feeding from a reference-data service, or a progressively-enhanced list; the drill-down tree is a UX choice, not a Design System component.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 17 main.html; legacy partials/consignment/commodityCodeSearch.html:33 includes consignment/commodityTree

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Chapter code button (e.g. '07') and chapter description button (e.g. 'EDIBLE VEGETABLES...') — both are type=submit buttons (name=parent_NN, value=NN) styled to look like links, that post the form to drill into that chapter.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Submit buttons styled as links — a11y/visual anti-pattern versus govuk-button or a real link. Uses form POST for navigation rather than links/GET.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: govuk Link (for navigation) or govuk Button (govuk-button--secondary) depending on semantics; ideally GET-based navigation for tree drill-down

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Presentational wrappers around the commodity code box, the description link, the row paragraph, and the breadcrumb root link within the tree
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Custom classes for layout/spacing of the bespoke tree; no GOV.UK equivalents. Only meaningful if the tree widget is retained.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: govuk spacing/typography utilities if a similar list is rebuilt

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: input[inputmode=numeric] used for commodity code, but name mismatch (id=commodity-code-search, name=commodity-text-input)
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: The search input accepts a commodity code; inputmode=numeric hints a numeric keypad on mobile though CN codes are entered as digits
nonStandardPatterns.concern: id/name mismatch is a legacy quirk; the field is a plain govuk-input, not an autocomplete (confirmed no combobox/autocomplete__wrapper present). Minor.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: Standard govuk Text input (as-is) or accessible-autocomplete if type-ahead is desired
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace b3742f19b6eea5d9285f5c8739571523a0a13ec1 action 16 (autocomplete=false); CORROBORATED legacy partials/consignment/commodityCodeSearch.html:14-20 — govuk/textInput partial with id='commodity-code-search' name='commodity-text-input' classes='govuk-!-width-one-third' labelText='Enter commodity code' inputmode='numeric'. The id/name mismatch is authored into the template, not a rendering artefact.

fields.name: commodity-text-input
fields.label: Enter commodity code
fields.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 17 (fill 10064000); trace b3742f19b6eea5d9285f5c8739571523a0a13ec1 action 16 (Search click navigates to page-4?commodity-selected-code=10064000). id=commodity-code-search, inputmode=numeric, class govuk-input govuk-!-width-one-third; HTML required attribute absent. CORROBORATED by page object SearchCommodityPage.ts:6-8 (getByRole textbox name 'Enter commodity code') and driven on every CHED-D journey at ched-d-workflows.ts:207 (fill) + :208 (Search click). Default value brokenRice=10064000 at ched-d-workflows.ts:87; copy-as-new default nutmeg=09081100 at ched-d-workflows.ts:564 (second observedValue inferred from test config, not directly seen in a trace). No HTML required attribute, but this input is the sole data-gathering control on the page and is filled on 100% of CHED-D journeys — de-facto required to progress. LEGACY: template partials/consignment/commodityCodeSearch.html:14-20 (govuk/textInput id/name/label/inputmode) confirms copy and control exactly. LEGACY mandatoriness: the input is NOT itself HTML-required; server-side Joi validation only requires it WHEN the Search button is pressed (action=search) — see validationMessages 'Enter a commodity code' / 'Commodity code must be a number'. validation/routes/handlers/importer/consignment_select.js:15-22.

fields.name: linkCommodityCodeSearch
fields.label: Commodity code search
fields.conditionalOn: ABSENT on CHED-D. The 'Commodity code search' / 'Species search' tab strip is rendered only when showTabs is true, and showTabs = [CVEDA, CVEDP, CHEDPP].includes(type) — CHED-D (type=CED) is NOT in that set. On CHED-D the plain commodityCodeSearch partial (code input + tree) is rendered directly with no tabs, so this tab-link does not exist. On CHED-A (CVEDA) / CHED-P (CVEDP) / CHED-PP the tabs show and the tests click this link to switch from the browse-tree/species view to code search.
fields.evidence: RESOLVED from legacy. routes/handlers/importer/consignment_select.js:206 (showTabs: [CVEDA, CVEDP, CHEDPP].includes(type)) and template views/importer/consignmentSelect.html:41-49 ({{#if showTabs}} ... commodityCodeSearchTab {{else}} commodityCodeSearch {{/if}}) prove the tabs — and therefore this 'Commodity code search' tab-link — are NOT present on CHED-D. The label copy 'Commodity code search' is from partials/consignment/commodityCodeSearchTab.html:9. Upgraded from the earlier 'inferred' page-object reading (SearchCommodityPage.ts:14-16): the CHED-D variant shows the code input by default and needs no view toggle. Prior page-object note retained: CHED-A/CHED-PP click this link first (ched-a-workflows.ts:272, ched-pp-workflows.ts:299) because their default view differs; CHED-D fills the input directly (ched-d-workflows.ts:207).

fields.name: parent_NN (submit buttons)
fields.label: Commodity tree chapter selection
options: 07 EDIBLE VEGETABLES AND CERTAIN ROOTS AND TUBERS
08 EDIBLE FRUIT AND NUTS; PEEL OF CITRUS FRUIT OR MELONS
11 PRODUCTS OF THE MILLING INDUSTRY; MALT; STARCHES; INULIN; WHEAT GLUTEN
12 OIL SEEDS AND OLEAGINOUS FRUITS; MISCELLANEOUS GRAINS, SEEDS AND FRUIT; INDUSTRIAL OR MEDICINAL PLANTS; STRAW AND FODDER
13 LAC; GUMS, RESINS AND OTHER VEGETABLE SAPS AND EXTRACTS
14 VEGETABLE PLAITING MATERIALS; VEGETABLE PRODUCTS NOT ELSEWHERE SPECIFIED OR INCLUDED
15 ANIMAL OR VEGETABLE FATS AND OILS AND THEIR CLEAVAGE PRODUCTS; PREPARED EDIBLE FATS; ANIMAL OR VEGETABLE WAXES
19 PREPARATIONS OF CEREALS, FLOUR, STARCH OR MILK; PASTRYCOOKS' PRODUCTS
20 PREPARATIONS OF VEGETABLES, FRUIT, NUTS OR OTHER PARTS OF PLANTS
23 RESIDUES AND WASTE FROM THE FOOD INDUSTRIES; PREPARED ANIMAL FODDER
fields.conditionalOn: Alternative to the code search — user browses the commodity tree instead of typing a code. Rendered via the commodityTree partial included from commodityCodeSearch.html:33; drill-down posts the form with a parent_NN field (value = chapter number).
fields.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 17 (root-level chapters shown). NOTE: this is reference data (the CN/commodity chapter tree) — the 16 chapters shown are the CHED-D-relevant top-level chapters at this snapshot, not a hardcoded list. The new app should source the commodity hierarchy from a reference-data service. control='autocomplete' is the nearest enum value; the real IPAFFS control is a bespoke drill-down tree of submit buttons (see nonStandardPatterns). LEGACY: the tree is a server-rendered widget (getTopLevelCommodityTree / getByParentCode in consignment_select.js:148-165, filtered by certType=CED); the drill-down click posts parent_NN and re-renders the same page one level deeper (consignment_select.js:62-67).

validationMessages.trigger: The 'Search' button is pressed (action=search) with the commodity code input left empty.
validationMessages.evidence: validation/routes/handlers/importer/consignment_select.js:15-22 (COMMODITY_TEXT_INPUT_KEY is Joi.number().empty('').required() when action=search; ANY_REQUIRED message = requiredMessage(COMMODITY_CODE, A)); message builder validation/messages/en.js:31-33 (`Enter ${article} ${label.toLowerCase()}`); label validation/labels/en.js:24 (COMMODITY_CODE='Commodity code') + :2 (A='a'). Resolves to 'Enter a commodity code'. This is the empty-search validation the traces never rendered (both traces filled a valid code).

validationMessages.message: Commodity code must be a number
validationMessages.trigger: The 'Search' button is pressed (action=search) with a non-numeric commodity code entered.
validationMessages.evidence: validation/routes/handlers/importer/consignment_select.js:19 (NUMBER_TYPE message = typeMessage(COMMODITY_CODE, NUMBER)); message builder validation/messages/en.js:27-29 (`${label} must be a ${type}`); labels validation/labels/en.js:24 (COMMODITY_CODE='Commodity code') + :71 (NUMBER='number'). Resolves to 'Commodity code must be a number'. Note the handler also silently ignores non-numeric input on the POST path (consignment_select.js:77 `!isNaN`); the Joi type message fires on the validate() branch.

validationMessages.message: You cannot add the same commodity code twice
validationMessages.trigger: The user selects (via code search or tree) a commodity code that is already present on the notification.
validationMessages.evidence: routes/handlers/importer/consignment_select.js:108-114 (commodityAlreadySelected → error { description: 'You cannot add the same commodity code twice', field: ['commodity'] }). Duplicate-commodity guard; unobserved in the two clean-path traces.

validationMessages.trigger: The user explicitly selects a commodity (selected-commodity payload) whose lookup does not resolve to a selectable commodity.
validationMessages.evidence: routes/handlers/importer/consignment_select.js:99-103 (handleCommoditySelected → error { description: 'Invalid commodityID', field: ['commodity'] } when isSearchResultSelectableAsCommodity is false). Looks like an internal/edge-case message rather than user-facing GDS copy — flag for the rebuild to reword or replace.

validationMessages.trigger: Model-level (submit-time) validation when the notification has no commodityComplement — i.e. no commodity was ever selected on this page.
validationMessages.evidence: ipaffs-imports-notification-schema Commodities.java:149-155 (@NotNull on List<CommodityComplement> commodityComplement, message key partone.euimp.commodities.commodityComplement.min.message); ValidationMessages.properties:91 (=A commodity must be selected). MANDATORINESS NOTE: this @NotNull sits on the NotificationLowRiskFieldValidation group, not a CED-named group, so its application to CHED-D at submit-time is a policy detail the rebuild should confirm — but functionally a commodity MUST be selected to progress the CHED-D journey (this page's entire purpose), so treat 'at least one commodity selected' as required.

validationMessages.trigger: Searching with an empty or invalid commodity code
validationMessages.evidence: ORIGINAL trace-pass gap, retained per no-downgrade rule. No error state was captured in either evidence trace (both fill a valid 8-digit code and advance cleanly). NOW LARGELY ANSWERED BY LEGACY: empty-search copy = 'Enter a commodity code' and non-numeric copy = 'Commodity code must be a number' (see the two legacy entries above). STILL A GAP: the 'code not found' / 'code resolves to a heading not a leaf commodity' cases — the handler re-renders the same page with no explicit error copy (consignment_select.js:87-90 falls through to pageView with no error when the search list is not a definitive commodity), so there is no distinct not-found message; a human should confirm the intended not-found behaviour in the rebuild.

openQuestions: No back link was present in main on this page (navigation is via the 'All commodities' breadcrumb + browser). Confirm whether a govuk Back link is expected in the rebuild.

RESOLVED (empty/non-numeric): legacy Joi validation supplies 'Enter a commodity code' (empty search) and 'Commodity code must be a number' (non-numeric). STILL OPEN: what should render when a numeric code is entered that does not resolve to a leaf commodity? Legacy IPAFFS silently re-renders the same page with no error (consignment_select.js:87-90) — decide whether the rebuild adds an explicit 'code not found' message.

The commodity tree is the dominant non-standard widget. Confirmed behaviour: typing a full 8-digit code (10064000 = rice/CEREALS ch.10) into the search field and pressing Search navigates straight to page-4 with ?commodity-selected-code=10064000 (i.e. selects the commodity and advances). The tree drill-down path (clicking chapter buttons) was not exercised in these two traces — its intermediate pages (chapter -> heading -> commodity leaf) are uncovered here and a lower bound.

Only 16 root chapters appear in this snapshot (07-15,17-21,23,39). Whether this list is CHED-D-filtered server-side or the full commodity root is unverified — legacy confirms the tree is fetched per certType (getTopLevelCommodityTree, consignment_select.js:152 with certType=CED), so the set is server-filtered reference data, not a fixed enum.

RESOLVED: the 'Commodity code search' tab-link is ABSENT on CHED-D. showTabs = [CVEDA, CVEDP, CHEDPP].includes(type) excludes CED (consignment_select.js:206), so CHED-D renders the plain commodityCodeSearch partial (code input + tree) directly with no tab strip and no species-search tab (consignmentSelect.html:41-49). The rebuild needs NO view toggle on the CHED-D variant.

MANDATORINESS: the commodity code input carries no HTML 'required' attribute; server-side Joi requires it only when Search is pressed (action=search) — 'Enter a commodity code'. At submit-time the model requires at least one commodityComplement ('A commodity must be selected', Commodities.java:149-155) but under the LowRiskFieldValidation group, not a CED-named group — confirm this group applies to CHED-D, or that an equivalent CED-group rule enforces 'at least one commodity selected'.

The 'You can only add {maxCommodityLines} commodity lines...' caption is explicitly HIDDEN on CHED-D (consignmentSelect.html:31 renders it only for `not isChedD`), consistent with the empty bodyCopy captured in the trace. Confirm the rebuild has no commodity-line cap messaging on the CHED-D search page.
